session.user: Change to /home/runner/work/erk/erk and execute: make gen-exec-reference-docs. Report all output. If that target doesn't exist, run `make help` to find the right target for regenerating exec reference docs.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show available make targets to find exec reference docs target

session.assistant: Now I can see there's no `gen-exec-reference-docs` target, but there is an `exec-reference-check` target. Looking at line 80, it runs `uv run erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs --check`. The actual command to generate (not check) the docs would be without the `--check` flag. Let me run that:

Perfect! The command executed successfully. Here's the output:
**Command executed:** `uv run erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs`
